the_formula.full_expansion:
S_{n+1} = [S_n] = S_n ⊕ (ΔS_n ⊗ M_n)
        
        Where:
          S_n = current epoch state (9-bit vector)
          S_{n+1} = next epoch state (9-bit vector)
          ΔS_n = S_{n+1} XOR S_n (desired state change)
          M_n = stability mask = NOT(C_bits(S_n)) AND NOT(C_bits(ΔS_n))
          C_bits = {b0, b1, b5} (Center-proximal bits that must never flip)
          ⊕ = XOR (binary addition without carry)
          ⊗ = AND (masking operation)
the_formula.recursive_step:
For n = 0, 1, 2, ..., 7:
          1. Compute ΔS_n = S_{n+1} XOR S_n
          2. Compute M_n = NOT(C_bits(S_n)) AND NOT(C_bits(ΔS_n))
          3. Compute S_{n+1} = S_n ⊕ (ΔS_n ⊗ M_n)
          4. Verify C(S_{n+1}) = 0 (Center preserved)

complete_epoch_sequence.description: The complete sequence of all 9 epochal states, computed recursively

verification.description: Verification that the recursive formula preserves C at each step
verification.conclusion: C(S_n) = 0 for all n ∈ {0,1,...,8}. The Center is preserved across all epochal transitions.

the_bracket.name: The Epochal Bracket
the_bracket.meaning: The bracket [S_n] represents the application of the Recursive Binary Operator to the current state. It is the operation that transforms one epoch into the next.

properties.closure: [S_n] always produces a valid epoch state
properties.determinism: [S_n] is uniquely determined by S_n (no randomness)
properties.reversibility: [S_n] is irreversible (XOR destroys information about previous states)
